{
  "name": "hypergraph removal lemma / arithmetic removal lemma",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": "math/0310476",
    "doi": "10.1007/s00039-005-0509-8",
    "citation": "B. Green, A Szemeredi-type regularity lemma in abelian groups, with applications, Geom. Funct. Anal. 15(2):340-376, 2005.",
    "year": 2005
  },
  "home_domain": "additive combinatorics",
  "what_it_does": "The arithmetic removal lemma turns a small count of linear configurations in finite abelian groups into a small deletion set that removes all such configurations. For 3-term progressions, its catalog signature concerns solutions of x+z=2y among specified subsets of a finite ambient group.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "a finite abelian ambient group G, or a finite interval modeled inside such a group",
    "three subsets X,Y,Z of G with a quantitative upper bound on solutions to x+z=2y",
    "the target configuration is expressible as the linear equation x+z=2y",
    "a removal-style conclusion, allowing deletion of o(|G|) or epsilon|G| elements from the input subsets"
  ],
  "output_signature": "If the number of 3-term AP solutions is sufficiently small, delete a small number of elements from each input set so that no such solutions remain.",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "a finite abelian ambient group G, or a finite interval modeled inside such a group",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: the target statement is a global finiteness question about a deterministic sequence in ℕ."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "three subsets X,Y,Z of G with a quantitative upper bound on solutions to x+z=2y",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: the statement provides no density or solution-count hypothesis over finite subsets."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "the target configuration is expressible as the linear equation x+z=2y",
      "match": "YES",
      "evidence": "Mathlib predicate Set.IsAPOfLength P.toSet 3 appears in the target statement."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a removal-style conclusion, allowing deletion of o(|G|) or epsilon|G| elements from the input subsets",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: the requested conclusion is finiteness of consecutive powerful-number triples, not AP elimination by deletion."
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "Powerful numbers have zero density, while arithmetic removal lemmas are density tools in finite ambient groups.",
    "The consecutive-term condition is an order constraint on the powerful-number sequence, not part of the linear equation x+z=2y.",
    "A removal conclusion does not directly produce finiteness of exact consecutive triples."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.25,
  "source_analog_id": "A5"
}
